data.content:
Implementation plan for scanned-agents refresh and Codex rename:

1. Refactor scanned selection state and mutations in src/components/settings-agents-hooks.tsx so scanned rows track multiple selected option ids per scanned agent instead of a single selected id. Update add/import helpers to add every selected option for a scanned CLI, preserve default-action assignment semantics for the first added id, and remove the now-obsolete bulk Add All pathway.

2. Rework scanned results UI in src/components/settings-agents-scanned.tsx and src/components/settings-agents-section.tsx:
- Remove per-row Add and registered badges from ScannedAgentRow, and remove the Add All header action entirely.
- Show the scanned CLI path beneath the agent/provider label when a path is present.
- Remove the lowercase agent.id text from renderOptionSelector.
- Replace the single-select control with a multi-select combobox/checklist that toggles option ids, keeps searchable filtering, and renders selected models as badges on the row’s right side.
- Keep the add action only at section level if still present, restyled to the page-header success treatment; restyle the Scan button to the lighter green Create More treatment.

3. Update provider naming in src/lib/agent-identity.ts and any direct vendor maps that key off the provider id. The detection path should still recognize codex/chatgpt/openai commands, but the returned provider id/label surface should read Codex instead of OpenAI. Review related UI consumers such as src/components/agent-info-bar.tsx and any helper text/comments that still mention OpenAI.

4. Adjust tests to match the real rename and UI surface. Minimum impacted files already confirmed in the repo are src/lib/__tests__/settings-scan.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/settings-coverage.test.ts, and src/components/__tests__/settings-agents-scanned.test.ts. A broader Codex rename sweep is likely needed in src/lib/__tests__/lease-audit.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/local-worker.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/settings-agents.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/settings-load.test.ts, src/lib/__tests__/setup-settings-writer.test.ts, plus any provider-label assertions reached through agent-identity helpers. Keep provider-id changes scoped carefully because lowercase analytics-style provider keys like openai in lease-audit view helpers may be unrelated to the settings identity layer.

5. Validation plan after implementation: run bun install --frozen-lockfile in the implementation worktree before checks, then run bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, and bun run test. Because the acceptance requires all three to pass, the implementation step should budget time for fixing any broken assertions uncovered by the Codex rename.

Complexity: medium. Main risks are (a) preserving import behavior when one scanned CLI exposes multiple selectable models, (b) avoiding accidental breakage in places that still intentionally use openai as a metrics/storage key, and (c) keeping the new row layout readable with long model labels and full filesystem paths.
